title: DRBUDDI for MRI Processing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ql2r","text":"This protocol will provide a basic guide to utilizing the DRBUDDI tool in TORTIOSE.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e346t","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2bt5","text":"Note: Steps may vary based upon image.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: DRBUDDI is a EPI distortion correction module in TORTOISE. It bases its corrections by using DTI data pairs (blip up and blip down). When processing MR images DRBUDDI is usually the second step in the pipeline.
In terminal go to your directory with the DTI data.
Type the following in the terminal to view information DR_BUDDI_withoutGUI
In terminal type: DR_BUDDI _withoutGUI --up _data directory(blip up)/file_name_proc.list --down_data directory(blip down)/file_name_proc.list --structural directory_T2Data/file_name_structural.nii
You should have two corresponding data sets to edit, and you will need to distinguish them when you call DRBUDDI i.e. blip up and blip down
NOTE: There are two DRBUDDI versions available to use. There is a DRBUDDI with a GUI, and there is one without a GUI.
creator: Courtney Comrie
